pages.authorityContentPage.takeAFullleng: Take a complet-longueur Examen simulé to assess your readiness and identifier faible zones.
pages.authorityGuidePages.05NoContractio: 0/5 (no contraction) to 5/5 (complet strength against resistance) bilaterally
pages.authorityGuidePages.2226MeqlMetabo: 22 - 26 mEq/L (composant métabolique — contrôlé par les reins)
pages.authorityGuidePages.300KetonesAbgM: 300), ketones, ABG (metabolic acidosis), Kussmaul breathing, fruity breath, déshydratation statut
pages.authorityGuidePages.30MlhrBeforeAd: 30 mL/hr) before administering magnesium sulfate. Loss of DTRs is the earliest signer of magnesium toxicité. The antidote is calcium gluconate — it must be at the bedside.
pages.authorityGuidePages.30MlhrCalciumG: 30 ml/h ; gluconate de calcium au chevet
pages.authorityGuidePages.3545MmhgRespir: 35 - 45 mmHg (respiratoire component — controlled by the poumons)
pages.authorityGuidePages.365375c977995f: 36,5-37,5 °C (97,7-99,5 °F) par voie orale. Le tympan et le temporal sont légèrement plus hauts que l'oral. Le rectal est de 0,5 à 1 °F plus élevé. L'axillaire est inférieur de 0,5 à 1 °F.
pages.authorityGuidePages.3SalineInfusio: 3% saline: perfusion pump, frequent Na⁺ surveillance, correct slowly
pages.authorityGuidePages.735745Below735: 7,35 - 7,45 (en dessous de 7,35 = acidose ; au-dessus de 7,45 = alcalose)
pages.authorityGuidePages.80100MmhgOxyge: 80 - 100 mmHg (oxygénation — not directly connexe to acid-base but critique for évaluation)
pages.authorityGuidePages.aSystematicHea: A systematic head-to-toe évaluation ensures no Système corporel is missed. While the ordre may vary between établissements, the principe of systematic évaluation is consistent. Activé examen questions, you need to know which assessments are relevant for each Système corporel and what anormal findings indicate.
pages.authorityGuidePages.abcsFirstThenV: ABCs premier, then Signes vitaux, douleur, chirurgical site (drainage, saignement), I&O, bowel sounds, mobility
pages.authorityGuidePages.acetazolamideD: Acetazolamide (Diamox) — a carbonic anhydrase inhibitor that causes rénal HCO₃⁻ wasting (rarely used)
pages.authorityGuidePages.activeAndPassi: Actif and passive plage of motion, crepitus, swelling
pages.authorityGuidePages.administerHypo: Administrer hypotonic liquides (0.45% NS or D5W) as ordered — correct gradually to prevent cerebral œdème
pages.authorityGuidePages.administerIvCa: Administrer IV calcium gluconate slowly (10-20 minutes) — moniteur for bradycardia and hypotension
pages.authorityGuidePages.afterAddressin: After addressing ABCs, prioritize physiological needs (oxygénation, nutrition, elimination, douleur) before sécurité, then psychosocial, then self-esteem, then self-actualization. Examen trap: a patient
pages.authorityGuidePages.afterThisInter: — "After this intervention, the infirmier(ère) should assess for..." or "Which finding indicates the traitement is efficace?"
pages.authorityGuidePages.albuterolNebul: Nébuliseur d'albutérol - déplace K⁺ dans les cellules par la stimulation du récepteur bêta-2
pages.authorityGuidePages.alertVerbalPai: Alerte, Verbal, Douleur, Unresponsive (AVPU) or Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS: 3-15)
pages.authorityGuidePages.allLungFieldsA: Tous poumon champs anteriorly and posteriorly — normal: vesicular, bronchial, bronchovesicular
pages.authorityGuidePages.alwaysAssessBe: Always assess before intervening. Activé examen questions, "assess" or "evaluate" is almost always the correct premier action unless the patient is in immediate danger (e.g., voies aériennes obstruction, cardiaque arrest, hémorragie). Even then, your évaluation is rapid — it just happens in secondes rather than minutes.
pages.authorityGuidePages.anionGapNaClHc: Espace anionique = Na⁺ - (Cl⁻ + HCO₃⁻) — Normal : 8-12 mEq/L
pages.authorityGuidePages.antidoteCalciu: Antidote: Calcium gluconate IV — garder at bedside during magnesium sulfate infusions
pages.authorityGuidePages.anuriaGrossHem: Anuria, gross hematuria, suprapubic distension, flank douleur with fièvre
pages.authorityGuidePages.anxietypanicAt: Anxiety/panic attacks (most courant), douleur, fièvre, early septicémie
pages.authorityGuidePages.anxietyrelated: Anxiety-connexe: calm reassurance, coached slow breathing, paper bag rebreathing (increases CO₂ inhalation) — use cautiously
pages.authorityGuidePages.arterialBloodG: Artériel blood gas (ABG) analyse provides a snapshot of a patient
pages.authorityGuidePages.assessAt45Dist: Assess at 45° — distension above 3 cm suggests right-sided cœur échec or liquide overload
pages.authorityGuidePages.assessForDigox: Assess for digoxin toxicité — hypokalemia increases sensitivity to digoxin
pages.authorityGuidePages.assessmentDiag: Évaluation → Diagnostic → Planning → Implementation → Évaluation. Activé examen questions, if you have not assessed the situation yet, évaluation is almost always the correct réponse. "Assess premier, then intervene" — unless there is an immediate life threat.
pages.authorityGuidePages.assessmentIsOn: Évaluation is only valuable if it leads to appropriate clinique decisions. Infirmier examens tester your ability to connecter évaluation findings to infirmier actions using established prioritization frameworks.
pages.authorityGuidePages.assessmentQues: Évaluation questions activé infirmier examens typically suivre one of four modèles:
pages.authorityGuidePages.asymmetricWeak: Asymmetric weakness, compartment syndrome signs, nouveau onset of unilateral swelling (DVT)
pages.authorityGuidePages.basedOnTheseAs: — "Based activé these évaluation findings, the infirmier(ère) suspects..." or "Which évaluation finding is expected for this condition?"
pages.authorityGuidePages.bicarbonateIsB: Bicarbonate is being lost from the corps:
pages.authorityGuidePages.breathSoundsCr: Breath sounds (crackles, bronchial), sputum (montant, color, consistency), temperature, SpO₂, WBC nombre
pages.authorityGuidePages.bronchodilator: Bronchodilatateurs (albutérol) pour le bronchospasme, aspiration au besoin pour éliminer les sécrétions
pages.authorityGuidePages.calcitoninBisp: Calcitonin, bisphosphonates (zoledronic acid) for malignancy-connexe hypercalcemia
pages.authorityGuidePages.calciumGlucona: Gluconate de calcium (antidote) + évaluer les DTR
Calcium gluconate IV — stabilizes cardiaque membrane (does NOT lower K⁺, protects the cœur)
Calcium gluconate: slow IV push (10-20 min), moniteur for bradycardia
pages.authorityGuidePages.calciumIsEssen: Calcium is essentiel for bone integrity, muscle contraction, blood clotting, and nerve transmission. Ionized (gratuit) calcium is the physiologically actif form. Always vérifier albumin niveaux — for every 1 g/dL decrease in albumin, total calcium decreases by approximately 0.8 mg/dL (corrected calcium calculation).
pages.authorityGuidePages.cardiacMonitor: Cardiaque surveillance + calcium gluconate (stabilize the cœur premier)
pages.authorityGuidePages.cardiacTampona: (cardiaque tamponade): Hypotension + JVD + Muffled cœur sounds → pericardiocentesis needed
pages.authorityGuidePages.checkMagnesium: Vérifier magnesium niveau — hypokalemia will not correct until hypomagnesemia is treated
pages.authorityGuidePages.chestPainIrreg: Chest douleur, irregular rhythm, absent pulses, cool/mottled extremities, JVD
pages.authorityGuidePages.chronicAlcohol: Chronique alcoholism (most courant), malnutrition, loop/thiazide diuretics, GI losses, proton pump inhibitors
pages.authorityGuidePages.clearPaleYello: Effacer, pale yellow to amber. Dark/concentrated = déshydratation. Cloudy/foul-smelling = infection. Pink/red = hematuria
pages.authorityGuidePages.clinicalAssess: Clinique évaluation questions comprise approximately 15-25% of infirmier licensing examens (NCLEX and REx-PN). These questions tester your ability to gather and interpret patient données, recognize anormal findings, and prioritize infirmier actions based activé évaluation résultats. The NCSBN Jugement clinique Measurement Modèle begins with "Recognize Cues" — which is fundamentally évaluation.
pages.authorityGuidePages.cnsDisordersHe: CNS troubles (head blessure, brain tumeur), salicylate (aspirin) toxicité (early)
pages.authorityGuidePages.coDecreases12M: Le CO₂ diminue de 1,2 mmHg pour chaque diminution de 1 mEq/L de HCO₃⁻ (hiver
pages.authorityGuidePages.coIncreases07M: Le CO₂ augmente de 0,7 mmHg pour chaque augmentation de 1 mEq/L de HCO₃⁻
pages.authorityGuidePages.coRetentionDue: Rétention de CO₂ due à une hypoventilation ou à un échange gazeux altéré. Le CO₂ se combine à l'eau pour former de l'acide carbonique, abaissant ainsi le pH.
pages.authorityGuidePages.colorTemperatu: Color, temperature, moisture, turgor (tester over sternum or forehead in elderly), integrity
pages.authorityGuidePages.confusionLetha: Confusion, lethargy, headache, nausea/vomiting, seizures (severe), cerebral œdème. Symptômes correlate with évaluer of refuser — aigu onset is plus dangerous than chronique.
pages.authorityGuidePages.confusionSeizu: (confusion, convulsions) → pensez à un déséquilibre sodique ou calcique sévère
pages.authorityGuidePages.constipationOr: (constipation ou diarrhée) → pensez au potassium ou au calcium
pages.authorityGuidePages.copdLowflowO12: COPD: faible-flow O₂ (1-2 L/min, cible SpO₂ 88-92%). Do NOT give élevé-flow O₂ — removes hypoxic respiratoire drive
pages.authorityGuidePages.copdMostCommon: COPD (most courant), obesity hypoventilation syndrome, kyphoscoliosis, chronique neuromuscular troubles
pages.authorityGuidePages.copdPatientsAr: COPD patients are chronique CO₂ retainers. Their respiratoire drive depends activé hypoxia, not CO₂ niveaux. Giving élevé-flow O₂ eliminates the hypoxic drive and can cause respiratoire arrest. Always use faible-flow O₂ targeting SpO₂ 88-92%.
pages.authorityGuidePages.correctSlowlyN: Correct slowly: no plus than 8-12 mEq/L per 24 heures to prevent osmotic demyelination syndrome (ODS)
pages.authorityGuidePages.cracklesFluida: Crackles (fluid/atelectasis), wheezes (bronchospasm), stridor (upper voies aériennes), pleural friction rub
pages.authorityGuidePages.cushingSyndrom: Syndrome de Cushing / excès de corticostéroïdes
pages.authorityGuidePages.d5wIsIsotonicI: D5W is isotonic in the bag but becomes hypotonic once infused (the dextrose is rapidly metabolized, leaving gratuit water). Therefore, D5W should NOT be used for patients with increased ICP or those at risque for cerebral œdème. This distinction is commonly tested.
pages.authorityGuidePages.dailyWeightSam: Daily poids (same temps, same scale, same clothing), I&O, poumon sounds (crackles), JVD, pedal œdème, activité tolerance
pages.authorityGuidePages.dataActionResp: Données, Action, Réponse — focused charting format
pages.authorityGuidePages.decreasedDtrsE: Decreased DTRs (earliest signer), lethargy, bradycardia, respiratoire depression, cardiaque arrest
pages.authorityGuidePages.diabetesInsipi: Diabetes insipidus, déshydratation, excessive sodium intake, hypertonic tube feedings, heatstroke, watery diarrhea without liquide replacement
pages.authorityGuidePages.dialysisDefini: Dialysis — definitive traitement for severe or refractory hyperkalemia
pages.authorityGuidePages.dialysisForSev: Dialyse en cas d'élévation sévère ou réfractaire
pages.authorityGuidePages.diarrheaMostCo: Diarrhea (most courant — GI tract loses HCO₃⁻)
pages.authorityGuidePages.dietarySources: Sources alimentaires : légumes verts feuillus, noix, légumineuses, grains entiers
pages.authorityGuidePages.diluteIvPotass: Potassium IV dilué : concentration maximale de 40 mEq/L pour IV périphérique, 80 mEq/L pour cathéter central
pages.authorityGuidePages.discontinueAll: Discontinue tous potassium supplements and potassium-sparing médicaments
pages.authorityGuidePages.diureticUseLoo: Utilisation de diurétiques (anse/thiazidique – provoque une alcalose de contraction par déplétion volémique)
pages.authorityGuidePages.doNotConfuseDi: Do not confuse dilutional hyponatremia (liquide overload, treat with liquide restriction) with depletional hyponatremia (sodium loss, treat with saline replacement). The traitement is opposite — getting this wrong activé the examen means choosing a harmful intervention.
pages.authorityGuidePages.doNotSuppressK: Do NOT suppress Kussmaul respirations — they are the corps
pages.authorityGuidePages.documentAsClos: Document as fermer to the temps of évaluation as possible. Late entries should be clearly identified
pages.authorityGuidePages.documentWhatYo: Document what you see, hear, mesurer, and observe — not your interpretation or judgment. Écrire "patient states
pages.authorityGuidePages.documentationI: Documentation is a legal enregistrer of Soins infirmiers and a competency tested activé les deux the NCLEX and REx-PN. Proper documentation of évaluation findings protects patients, supports continuity of soins, and provides legal defense. Examen questions may ask you to identifier correct documentation practices or recognize documentation erreurs.
pages.authorityGuidePages.drainageAmount: Quantité/couleur du drainage, marée dans le joint hydraulique, emphysème sous-cutané, bruits respiratoires bilatéraux, intégrité du site de la sonde
pages.authorityGuidePages.ecgChangesDysr: (modifications de l'ECG, dysrythmies) → penser au potassium ou au calcium
pages.authorityGuidePages.eitherExcessiv: Soit une perte excessive d'acide (H⁺), soit une rétention/administration excessive de base (HCO₃⁻).
pages.authorityGuidePages.eitherTooMuchA: Soit trop de production/ingestion d'acide, soit trop de perte de bicarbonate. Utilisez le trou anionique pour différencier.
pages.authorityGuidePages.electrolyteImb: Électrolyte imbalances rarely occur in isolation. Hypomagnesemia often accompanies hypokalemia. Hypocalcemia frequently coexists with hypomagnesemia. When you see one imbalance activé an examen question, look for autres that commonly co-occur — this modèle recognition is exactly what the NCLEX tests.
pages.authorityGuidePages.electrolyteQue: Électrolyte questions appear activé every infirmier licensing examen — NCLEX-RN, NCLEX-PN, and REx-PN. They tester your ability to recognize imbalances from clinique données, prioritize infirmier interventions, and understand the physiopathologie behind traitement decisions. The NCSBN tester plan places Électrolytes primarily under Physiological Adaptation and Reduction of Risque Potential, which together compte for 20-32% of the NCLEX-RN examen.
Électrolyte questions activé the NCLEX and REx-PN suivre predictable modèles. Knowing these modèles helps you réponse efficiently and accurately, even when the Scénario clinique is unfamiliar.
pages.authorityGuidePages.encourageMobil: Encourage mobility, moniteur for rénal calculi, strain urine
pages.authorityGuidePages.equalStrengthB: Force égale bilatéralement, sensation intacte, démarche/équilibre
pages.authorityGuidePages.excessiveCoEli: Élimination excessive de CO₂ due à l’hyperventilation. La perte de CO₂ (acide) augmente le pH.
pages.authorityGuidePages.excessiveSodiu: Administration excessive de bicarbonate de sodium
pages.authorityGuidePages.facialSymmetry: Symétrie faciale (CN VII), déglutition (CN IX/X), clarté de la parole
pages.authorityGuidePages.fastFaceDroopi: FAST (Face drooping, Arm weakness, Speech difficulté, Temps) + NIH AVC Scale, temps of symptôme onset
pages.authorityGuidePages.firstlineForDe: — premier-line for déshydratation, blood transfusions, and choc resuscitation. Does not shift liquide between compartments.
pages.authorityGuidePages.flattenedTWave: Ondes T aplaties, dépression ST, ondes U proéminentes, intervalle QT prolongé
pages.authorityGuidePages.fluidRestricti: Liquide restriction (dilutional hyponatremia / SIADH) — typically 800-1000 mL/day
pages.authorityGuidePages.focusedAssessm: Focused assessments are targeted evaluations performed when you know (or suspect) a spécifique condition. Examen questions often present a Scénario clinique and ask "Which évaluation is the priorité?" Knowing the focused évaluation for each condition is essentiel.
pages.authorityGuidePages.forDiabetesIns: For diabetes insipidus: administrer desmopressin (DDAVP) as ordered
pages.authorityGuidePages.forEachElectro: For each Électrolyte, know the single most important infirmier action:
pages.authorityGuidePages.gcsTrendingPup: Tendances GCS, réactivité des élèves et égalité, Cushing
pages.authorityGuidePages.hcoDecreases2M: HCO₃⁻ diminue de 2 mEq/L pour chaque baisse de 10 mmHg de CO₂
pages.authorityGuidePages.hcoIncreases1M: HCO₃⁻ augmente de 1 mEq/L pour chaque augmentation de 10 mmHg de CO₂
pages.authorityGuidePages.hcoIncreases35: HCO₃⁻ augmente de 3,5 mEq/L pour chaque augmentation de 10 mmHg de CO₂
pages.authorityGuidePages.headacheConfus: Headache, confusion, drowsiness progressing to coma, warm flushed skin (CO₂ vasodilation), tachycardia, dysrhythmias. In chronique COPD: elevated HCO₃⁻ indicates rénal compensation.
pages.authorityGuidePages.hyperaldostero: Hyperaldostéronisme (rétention de sodium, excrétion de H⁺ et K⁺)
pages.authorityGuidePages.hyperparathyro: Hyperparathyroïdie, tumeur maligne (métastases osseuses), immobilisation prolongée, excès de vitamine D, diurétiques thiazidiques
pages.authorityGuidePages.hypertonicSali: Hypertonic saline (3% NaCl) for severe symptomatic hyponatremia with seizures — administrer via perfusion pump with frequent Na⁺ surveillance
pages.authorityGuidePages.hypoparathyroi: Hypoparathyroidism, chronique kidney maladie, pancreatitis, inadequate vitamin D, hypomagnesemia, massive blood transfusions (citrate binds calcium), thyroidectomy
pages.authorityGuidePages.hypotensionBra: Hypotension + Bradycardia (differs from autre choc types which afficher tachycardia)
pages.authorityGuidePages.hypotensionTac: Hypotension + Tachycardia + Tachypnea + Altered mental statut → immediate intervention
pages.authorityGuidePages.iabeticKetoaci: Acidocétose iabétique (ACD) – la plus couramment testée
pages.authorityGuidePages.ifHypoxiadrive: If hypoxia-driven: treat the hypoxemia (oxygène thérapie, PE gestion)
pages.authorityGuidePages.ifTheActualCom: If the actual compensation is plus or less than expected, a mixed trouble is present. For exemple, a patient with DKA (metabolic acidosis) and pneumonia (respiratoire acidosis) would have les deux faible HCO₃⁻ AND élevé CO₂ — the pH would be very faible because les deux systèmes are contributing to acidosis.
pages.authorityGuidePages.inClinicalPrac: In Pratique clinique and activé avancé examen questions, patients may have two or plus acid-base troubles simultaneously. Recognizing mixed troubles requires understanding expected compensation ranges.
pages.authorityGuidePages.inFullyCompens: In fully compensated ABGs, the pH is normal but les deux CO₂ and HCO₃⁻ are anormal. To determine the primaire trouble, look at which side of 7.40 the pH falls. If pH is 7.35-7.39, the primaire trouble is acidosis. If pH is 7.41-7.45, the primaire trouble is alkalosis.
pages.authorityGuidePages.includeRelevan: Inclure relevant negatives. "No JVD, no peripheral edema" is clinically important information
pages.authorityGuidePages.increasedIcpHy: (increased ICP): Hypertension (widening pouls pressure) + Bradycardia + Irregular respirations → neurosurgical Urgence
pages.authorityGuidePages.increasedIntra: Increased intracranial pressure (worsens cerebral œdème), burns (third-spacing), hypovolemia
pages.authorityGuidePages.inspectAuscult: Inspecter, ausculter, percuter, palper (ausculter AVANT la palpation pour éviter de stimuler les bruits intestinaux)
pages.authorityGuidePages.intenseThirstD: Intense thirst, dry mucous membranes, flushed skin, faible-note fièvre, restlessness, irritability, muscle twitching, seizures (severe)
pages.authorityGuidePages.isTheOtherValu: Is the autre valeur also anormal? If yes, the corps is compensating. If the autre valeur is normal, the trouble is uncompensated.
pages.authorityGuidePages.isotonicInTheB: — isotonic in the bag, becomes hypotonic once dextrose is metabolized. Used for médicament dilution and maintenance hydration.
pages.authorityGuidePages.isotonicSoluti: Solutions isotoniques (même osmolalité que le sang : ~275-295 mOsm/L)
pages.authorityGuidePages.ivCalciumGluco: IV calcium gluconate + voies aériennes equipment at bedside
pages.authorityGuidePages.ivFluidResusci: IV liquide resuscitation (isotonic crystalloid for DKA and septicémie)
pages.authorityGuidePages.ivMagnesiumSul: IV magnesium sulfate for severe depletion — administrer slowly, moniteur DTRs, respiratoire évaluer, and urine sortie
pages.authorityGuidePages.ivNormalSaline: Solution saline IV normale pour l'alcalose sensible au chlorure (restaure le volume et le chlorure)
IV normal saline hydration (aggressive) — dilutes calcium and promotes rénal excretion
pages.authorityGuidePages.ivPotassiumAlw: Potassium IV : toujours dilué, toujours par pompe, jamais par poussée
pages.authorityGuidePages.ivPotassiumNev: IV potassium: NEVER give by IV push — maximum évaluer 10-20 mEq/hour via perfusion pump with cardiaque surveillance
pages.authorityGuidePages.ivPotassiumPus: IV potassium push causes fatal cardiaque arrest. This is one of the most commonly tested sécurité questions activé the NCLEX. Always administrer IV potassium diluted and infused slowly with cardiaque surveillance. Never ajouter KCl to a hanging IV bag — always use premixed solutions or perfusion pump.
pages.authorityGuidePages.ivSodiumOrPota: Phosphate de sodium ou de potassium IV en cas de carence grave – perfuser lentement
pages.authorityGuidePages.kayexalateSodi: Kayexalate (polystyrène sulfonate de sodium) – échange le sodium contre du potassium dans l'intestin (apparition retardée)
pages.authorityGuidePages.keepEmergencyA: Garder Urgence voies aériennes equipment at bedside (risque of laryngospasm)
pages.authorityGuidePages.kussmaulRespir: Kussmaul respirations (deep rapid breathing — respiratoire compensation), fruity breath (DKA), confusion, lethargy, warm flushed skin, nausea/vomiting, hyperkalemia (H⁺/K⁺ exchange)
pages.authorityGuidePages.lightheadednes: Étourdissements, étourdissements, picotements/engourdissements (circumoraux et extrémités), crampes musculaires, tétanie (l'alcalose diminue le calcium ionisé), palpitations, syncope.
pages.authorityGuidePages.loopAndThiazid: Loop and thiazide diuretics (most courant), vomiting, NG suction, diarrhea, alkalosis, insuline administration, excessive laxative use
pages.authorityGuidePages.loopDiureticsF: Diurétiques de l'anse (furosémide) après une hydratation adéquate – favorisent la calciurèse
pages.authorityGuidePages.lowphosphorusD: Faible-phosphorus diet: limit dairy, cola, processed meats, nuts
pages.authorityGuidePages.magnesiumIsCri: Magnesium is critique for neuromuscular fonction and is closely linked to potassium and calcium balance. Hypomagnesemia must be corrected before hypokalemia will respond to traitement — this is a élevé-yield examen concept.
pages.authorityGuidePages.measureBpAndHr: Mesurer BP and HR supine, then standing (or sitting if Impossible de stand). Positif orthostatic hypotension: SBP drop ≥ 20 mmHg OR DBP drop ≥ 10 mmHg OR HR increase ≥ 20 bpm within 3 minutes. Indicates volume depletion or autonomic dysfunction. Infirmier action: ensure sécurité (fall precautions), rapport to fournisseur, hold antihypertensives as appropriate.
pages.authorityGuidePages.mechanicalVent: Mechanical ventilation (évaluer or tidal volume définir too élevé)
Mechanical ventilation: reduce évaluer or tidal volume as ordered
pages.authorityGuidePages.monitorDtrsBef: Moniteur DTRs before each magnesium sulfate dose (hold if absent)
pages.authorityGuidePages.monitorEcgCont: Moniteur ECG continuously during IV replacement
pages.authorityGuidePages.monitorEcgForD: Moniteur ECG for dysrhythmias connexe to hypokalemia
pages.authorityGuidePages.monitorForAsso: Moniteur for associated hypocalcemia and hypomagnesemia
pages.authorityGuidePages.monitorForHypo: Moniteur for hypoventilation and hypoxemia (respiratoire compensation)
pages.authorityGuidePages.monitorForTeta: Moniteur for tetany and cardiaque dysrhythmias (alkalosis-connexe hypocalcemia)
pages.authorityGuidePages.monitorNeuroSt: Moniteur neuro statut every 1-2 heures — confusion, lethargy, and seizures indicate worsening
pages.authorityGuidePages.monitorPotassi: Moniteur potassium closely — as acidosis corrects, K⁺ shifts retour into cells causing hypokalemia
pages.authorityGuidePages.monitorRespira: Moniteur respiratoire évaluer, depth, and modèle every 1-2 heures
pages.authorityGuidePages.monitorUrineSp: Moniteur urine spécifique gravity (normal: 1.005-1.030) — faible in DI, élevé in déshydratation
pages.authorityGuidePages.muscleWeakness: Muscle weakness, leg cramps, decreased DTRs, constipation/ileus, fatigue, cardiaque dysrhythmias
Muscle weakness progressing to flaccid paralysis, paresthesias, abdominal cramping, diarrhea, cardiaque arrest
Muscle weakness, respiratoire échec (diaphragm weakness), confusion, seizures, rhabdomyolysis, impaired WBC fonction
pages.authorityGuidePages.neuroChecksEve: Neuro checks every 1-2 heures, drainage (CSF vs blood), plaie site, seizure precautions
pages.authorityGuidePages.neurovascularC: Neurovascular checks, abduction pillow/positioning, DVT prévention, douleur gestion, mobility progression
pages.authorityGuidePages.newPressureInj: Nouveau pressure blessure, signs of plaie infection, crepitus (subcutaneous emphysema), cyanosis
pages.authorityGuidePages.newonsetAcuteF: Nouveau-onset (aigu) findings generally take priorité over chronic/stable findings. A patient with nouveau-onset confusion is a higher priorité than a patient with known chronique douleur, even if the chronique douleur patient is uncomfortable.
pages.authorityGuidePages.normal530minIn: Normal: 5-30/min in tous four quadrants. Hyperactive: diarrhea, early obstruction. Hypoactive/absent: ileus, publier-operative
pages.authorityGuidePages.normalAnionGap: Acidose métabolique à trou anionique normal (hyperchlorémique)
pages.authorityGuidePages.oftenConsidere: Often considered the "5th vital signer." Use appropriate scale for patient population.
pages.authorityGuidePages.onDocumentatio: Activé documentation questions, choisir the most objectif, spécifique, and measurable option. Avoid réponses that inclure infirmier opinions, subjective interpretations, or vague conditions like "bon," "normal," or "improved" without measurable criteria.
pages.authorityGuidePages.opioidOverdose: Opioid overdose: administrer naloxone (Narcan) — moniteur for re-sedation
pages.authorityGuidePages.opioidsedative: Opioid/sedative overdose, pneumothorax, voies aériennes obstruction, neuromuscular maladie (Guillain-Barré, myasthenia gravis), severe asthma, pneumonia, ARDS, anesthesia
pages.authorityGuidePages.oralCalciumSup: Oral calcium supplements with vitamin D for chronique gestion
pages.authorityGuidePages.oralPhosphorus: Suppléments oraux de phosphore (Neutra-Phos) aux repas
pages.authorityGuidePages.oralPotassiumR: Oral potassium replacement preferred (KCl tablets with complet glass of water and food to reduce GI irritation)
pages.authorityGuidePages.painAssessment: Douleur évaluation (PQRST), Signes vitaux, ECG surveillance, troponin niveaux, O₂ saturation
pages.authorityGuidePages.peakFlowMeasur: Peak flow measurement, travail of breathing, breath sounds (wheezing, silent chest = severe), SpO₂, ability to speak in complet sentences
pages.authorityGuidePages.perrlaPupilsEq: PERRLA (Pupils Equal, Round, Reactive to Light and Accommodation) — unequal or fixed/dilated = Urgence
pages.authorityGuidePages.personPlaceTim: Person, place, temps, situation (documented as "A&Ox4" or partial)
pages.authorityGuidePages.ph728Paco55Hco: pH 7.28, PaCO₂ 55, HCO₃⁻ 24 → Uncompensated respiratoire acidosis (pH faible, CO₂ élevé, HCO₃⁻ normal). Think: COPD exacerbation, opioid overdose, or pneumonia.
pages.authorityGuidePages.ph732Paco30Hco: pH 7.32, PaCO₂ 30, HCO₃⁻ 15 → Partially compensated metabolic acidosis (pH faible, HCO₃⁻ faible, CO₂ faible from respiratoire compensation). Think: DKA, lactic acidosis, or rénal échec.
pages.authorityGuidePages.ph748Paco28Hco: pH 7.48, PaCO₂ 28, HCO₃⁻ 24 → Uncompensated respiratoire alkalosis (pH élevé, CO₂ faible, HCO₃⁻ normal). Think: anxiety, douleur, PE, or early septicémie.
pages.authorityGuidePages.ph750Paco46Hco: pH 7.50, PaCO₂ 46, HCO₃⁻ 34 → Partially compensated metabolic alkalosis (pH élevé, HCO₃⁻ élevé, CO₂ slightly elevated from respiratoire compensation). Think: vomiting, NG suctioning, or diuretic use.
pages.authorityGuidePages.phIsAbnormalOn: pH is anormal, only one valeur (CO₂ or HCO₃⁻) is anormal. The corps has not yet begun to compensate.
pages.authorityGuidePages.phIsDetermined: pH is determined by the ratio of HCO₃⁻ to CO₂. Normal ratio is 20:1 (bicarbonate to carbonic acid). Any processus that changes this ratio will alter the pH.
pages.authorityGuidePages.phIsStillAbnor: pH is still anormal, but les deux CO₂ and HCO₃⁻ are anormal. The corps is trying to compensate but has not fully corrected the pH.
pages.authorityGuidePages.phIsWithinNorm: pH is within Plage normale (7.35-7.45), but les deux CO₂ and HCO₃⁻ are anormal. Look at which end of normal the pH falls to determine the primaire trouble.
pages.authorityGuidePages.phosphatebindi: Phosphate-binding médicaments (calcium carbonate, sevelamer) — administrer WITH meals to bind dietary phosphorus
pages.authorityGuidePages.phosphorusHasA: Phosphorus has an inverse relationship with calcium — when one goes up, the autre goes down. This reciprocal relationship is a frequently tested concept. Phosphorus is essentiel for ATP production, bone mineralization, and acid-base buffering.
pages.authorityGuidePages.potassiumIsThe: Potassium is the most abundant intracellular cation and is critique for cardiaque conduction, neuromuscular fonction, and acid-base balance. Les deux hypokalemia and hyperkalemia can cause life-threatening cardiaque dysrhythmias, making potassium the most frequently tested Électrolyte activé infirmier examens. ECG changes are a priorité évaluation finding.
pages.authorityGuidePages.practiceWithEx: Pratique with examen-style questions and détaillé clinique rationales.
pages.authorityGuidePages.preferredForSu: — preferred for chirurgical patients and burns (Parkland formule). Contains Électrolytes (Na, K, Ca, lactate). Contraindicated in foie échec (cannot metabolize lactate) and hyperkalemia.
pages.authorityGuidePages.prepareForPoss: Préparer for possible intubation/mechanical ventilation if PaCO₂ continues to rise
pages.authorityGuidePages.rate1220minDep: Évaluer (12-20/min), depth, modèle (regular, Cheyne-Stokes, Kussmaul, Biot
pages.authorityGuidePages.readTheLabValu: Lire the Valeurs de laboratoire carefully. Identifier which Électrolyte is anormal and whether it is élevé or faible. Many étudiants make erreurs because they focus activé the Scénario clinique before checking the labs.
pages.authorityGuidePages.refeedingSyndr: Refeeding syndrome is a life-threatening complication of rapidly restarting nutrition in malnourished patients. As cells take up glucose, phosphorus, potassium, and magnesium shift intracellularly causing dangerous drops. This is heavily tested in contexte of eating trouble patients and prolonged NPO statut.
Refeeding syndrome (most commonly tested), chronique alcoholism, diabetic ketoacidosis récupération (insuline drives PO₄ into cells), phosphate-binding antacids, respiratoire alkalosis
Refeeding syndrome prévention: advance nutrition gradually in malnourished patients
pages.authorityGuidePages.regularInsulin: Regular insuline + D50 IV — drives K⁺ into cells (temporary, moniteur glucose closely)
pages.authorityGuidePages.regulateCoLeve: Regulate CO₂ niveaux within minutes. Hyperventilation blows désactivé CO₂ (raises pH). Hypoventilation retains CO₂ (lowers pH). CO₂ is an acid when dissolved in blood (forms carbonic acid, H₂CO₃).
pages.authorityGuidePages.regulateHcoBic: Regulate HCO₃⁻ (bicarbonate) niveaux over heures to jours. The kidneys can either retain or excrete bicarbonate and hydrogen ions. This is a slower but plus sustained compensatory mécanisme.
pages.authorityGuidePages.reinforceKeyCo: Reinforce Concepts clés with spaced-repetition cartes mémoire.
pages.authorityGuidePages.rememberStones: Remember "stones, bones, groans, moans" — kidney stones, bone douleur, abdominal pain/constipation, confusion/lethargy. Shortened QT, cardiaque arrest at severe niveaux.
pages.authorityGuidePages.renalFailureCa: Rénal échec (cannot excrete), excessive magnesium-containing antacids/laxatives, preeclampsia traitement (magnesium sulfate toxicité)
Rénal échec (cannot excrete), excessive phosphorus intake, hypoparathyroidism, tumeur lysis syndrome, rhabdomyolysis
pages.authorityGuidePages.renalFailureMo: Rénal échec (most courant), potassium-sparing diuretics, ACE inhibitors, tissu trauma/burns, acidosis, hemolyzed blood échantillon (false elevation)
pages.authorityGuidePages.replacePotassi: Remplacer potassium (hypokalemia is les deux a cause and consequence of metabolic alkalosis)
Remplacer potassium (NEVER IV push) + moniteur ECG
pages.authorityGuidePages.respiratorySta: Respiratoire statut (voies aériennes swelling), voice qualité, Chvostek
pages.authorityGuidePages.rigidboardlike: Abdomen rigide/en forme de planche, sensibilité au rebond, absence de bruits intestinaux, hématémèse, méléna
pages.authorityGuidePages.s1Mitraltricus: S1 (mitral/tricuspid closure), S2 (aortic/pulmonic closure), S3 (HF indicateur in adults), S4 (decreased compliance), murmurs
pages.authorityGuidePages.scale1To4Pitti: Scale 1+ to 4+ pitting, emplacement, onset modèle (bilateral vs unilateral)
pages.authorityGuidePages.seizurePrecaut: Précautions contre les convulsions, précautions contre les chutes (irritabilité neuromusculaire)
pages.authorityGuidePages.shallowSlowRes: Shallow slow respirations (compensatory hypoventilation), confusion, irritability, muscle cramps, tetany (alkalosis decreases ionized calcium), hypokalemia (H⁺/K⁺ exchange), cardiaque dysrhythmias
pages.authorityGuidePages.siadhMostCommo: SIADH (most courant examen cause), excessive hypotonic IV liquides, cœur échec, cirrhosis, psychogenic polydipsia, thiazide diuretics, vomiting/diarrhea with water replacement
pages.authorityGuidePages.signsOfBleedin: Signs of saignement (bruising, gum saignement, dark stools, hematuria), PTT/INR niveaux
pages.authorityGuidePages.similarToHypoc: Similar to hypocalcemia — tremors, hyperactive DTRs, positif Trousseau
pages.authorityGuidePages.situationBackg: Situation, Background, Évaluation, Recommandation — used for fournisseur communication
pages.authorityGuidePages.sizeDepthDrain: Size, depth, drainage (serous, sanguineous, purulent), plaie bed color, periwound skin
pages.authorityGuidePages.sodiumBicarbon: Bicarbonate de sodium IV - déplace K⁺ intracellulaire en cas d'acidose métabolique
pages.authorityGuidePages.sodiumIsTheMos: Sodium is the most abundant extracellular cation and the primaire determinant of serum osmolality. Sodium imbalances are fundamentally water balance troubles — hyponatremia usually reflects water excess, while hypernatremia reflects water deficit.
pages.authorityGuidePages.softNontenderN: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Rebound tenderness = peritoneal irritation (Urgence)
pages.authorityGuidePages.somethingIsAdd: Something is adding acid to the corps:
pages.authorityGuidePages.stage1Nonblanc: Étape 1 (non-blanchable erythema) through Étape 4 (complet-thickness with exposed bone/tendon), Unstageable, DTPI
pages.authorityGuidePages.strategy2Match: Stratégie 2: Associer Signs to the Imbalance
pages.authorityGuidePages.strictIoDailyW: Strict I&O, daily weights, sécurité measures for confusion
I&O strictes, poids quotidiens, précautions contre les crises
pages.authorityGuidePages.subjectiveObje: Subjective, Objectif, Évaluation, Plan — problème-oriented charting
pages.authorityGuidePages.suddenUnilater: Faiblesse unilatérale soudaine, troubles de l'élocution, maux de tête sévères, modifications de la pupille, diminution du GCS
pages.authorityGuidePages.tallPeakedTWav: Tall peaked T waves → widened QRS → absent P waves → sine wave modèle → ventricular fibrillation/asystole
pages.authorityGuidePages.temperatureHyp: Température (hyperthermie), FC (tachycardie sévère), agitation, délire, transpiration
pages.authorityGuidePages.theMostFundame: The most fondamental prioritization framework. Always adresse voies aériennes problèmes premier, then breathing, then circulation. Activé the NCLEX, if one patient has an voies aériennes problème and another has a circulation problème, the voies aériennes patient is always the priorité.
pages.authorityGuidePages.theNclexLovesP: The NCLEX loves Sécurité du patient questions involving Électrolytes:
pages.authorityGuidePages.theRomeMethodI: The ROME méthode is the most reliable systematic approach to ABG interpretation activé infirmier examens. It eliminates guesswork and works for every ABG scénario.
pages.authorityGuidePages.toSucceedWithE: To succeed with Électrolyte questions, you need to understand three things for each Électrolyte: (1) the Plage normale and what drives it up or down, (2) the clinique signs and symptômes of les deux excess and deficit, and (3) the priorité infirmier interventions including surveillance, médicament administration, and Sécurité du patient measures.
pages.authorityGuidePages.treatTheUnderl: Treat the underlying cause: antiemetics for vomiting, remplacer NG losses with appropriate liquides
Treat the underlying cause (insuline for DKA, antibiotiques for septicémie, dialysis for rénal échec)
pages.authorityGuidePages.treatUnderlyin: Treat underlying cause: analgesics for douleur, antipyretics for fièvre, anxiolytics for anxiety
pages.authorityGuidePages.understandingI: Understanding IV liquide tonicity is essentiel for managing Électrolyte imbalances safely. Selecting the wrong IV liquide can worsen the imbalance and cause harm — this is why IV liquide questions are courant activé infirmier examens.
pages.authorityGuidePages.unexpectedFind: Unexpected findings exiger action; expected findings exiger surveillance. If a publier-chirurgical patient rapports mild incisional douleur (expected), that is lower priorité than a patient reporting sudden chest tightness (unexpected). Know Qu
pages.authorityGuidePages.unstablePatien: Unstable patients always take priorité. Stable patients can attendre, can often be delegated, and are appropriate for infirmier assistants to moniteur. Unstable patients exiger RN évaluation and intervention.
pages.authorityGuidePages.useApprovedAbb: Use approuvé abbreviations only. If you make an erreur, draw a single line through it, écrire "erreur," initial, and date — never use white-out or scribble over
pages.authorityGuidePages.useMeasurableT: Use measurable conditions. "Plaie 3 cm x 2 cm with serous drainage" is better than "plaie looks good"
pages.authorityGuidePages.usedForHyperna: — used for hypernatremia, cellular déshydratation. Liquide shifts FROM vasculaire space INTO cells.
pages.authorityGuidePages.usedForSevereS: — used for severe symptomatic hyponatremia with seizures. Liquide shifts FROM cells INTO vasculaire space.
pages.authorityGuidePages.viaInfusionPum: Via perfusion pump only, central line preferred, frequent sodium surveillance (every 2-4 heures)
pages.authorityGuidePages.vitalSignsAreT: Signes vitaux are the most basique and most critique évaluation données. Knowing normal ranges, understanding what deviations mean, and recognizing life-threatening vital signer modèles is essentiel for les deux examen succès and Pratique clinique.
pages.authorityGuidePages.vomitingngSuct: Vomissements/aspiration de NG (perte de HCl de l’estomac – le plus souvent testé)
pages.authorityGuidePages.whenAnExamQues: When an examen question presents multiple patients with anormal Signes vitaux, use the ABCs (Voies aériennes, Breathing, Circulation) to prioritize. A patient with respiratoire distress (SpO₂ 82%, RR 32) takes priorité over a patient with fièvre (38.8°C) or mild tachycardia (HR 105).
pages.authorityGuidePages.whichAssessmen: — "Which évaluation finding indicates..." or "The infirmier(ère) should assess for which complication?"
— "Which évaluation finding requires immediate intervention?" or "Which patient should the infirmier(ère) see premier?"
pages.authorityGuidePages.whichValueCoOr: Which valeur (CO₂ or HCO₃⁻) explains the pH direction? That is the primaire trouble.

pages.bloodTransfusionSimulator.noAboAnt: No ABO antibodies and Rh+; can recevoir any RBC type
Pas d'antigènes ABO, possède des anticorps anti-A et anti-B
pages.bloodTransfusionSimulator.noDAntig: No D antigen; cannot recevoir Rh+ blood
pages.bloodTransfusionSimulator.oPrbcsAn: O- PRBCs and AB FFP when no temps for crossmatch
pages.bloodTransfusionSimulator.oUnivers: O− = Donneur universel de globules rouges
pages.bloodTransfusionSimulator.plasmaHa: Plasma has tous ABO antibodies, can recevoir any
Le plasma ne contient pas d'anticorps anti-A ou anti-B
pages.bloodTransfusionSimulator.rhPatien: Rh+ patients can recevoir Rh+ or Rh- blood
Rh- patients must recevoir Rh- blood only

pages.healthcarePolicyHub.stayInformedOn: Stay informed activé soins de santé politique changes affecting nurses. Evergreen guides activé licensing politique mises à jour, international infirmier recruitment, Format d
pages.healthcarePolicyHub.understandingH: Understanding soins de santé politique changes helps you stay compliant, advance your carrière, and advocate for better Soins aux patients.
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.canadianNurs: Canadian infirmier examens, including the REX-PN and NCLEX-RN (used by most Canadian provinces for RN licensure), suivre similar CAT methodologies but may have jurisdiction-spécifique contenu emphases. The transition from the CPNRE to the REX-PN for practical infirmier marked a significant format shift toward the CAT modèle.
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.catTechnolog: CAT technology ensures each candidate receives a uniquely tailored examen. The algorithm selects questions based activé your demonstrated ability niveau, making the examen les deux efficient and precise. Understanding how CAT works helps reduce tester anxiety and inform your tester-taking stratégie.
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.commonPathwa: Courant pathways inclure employer-sponsored travail visas, skilled worker programmes (such as Canada
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.comprehensiv: Complet guide to soins de santé regulatory changes affecting Pratique infirmière. Covers staffing ratio laws, Sécurité du patient regulations, telehealth standards, continuing éducation mandates, and workplace sécurité exigences.
Complet guide to infirmier licensure politique changes including Infirmier(ère) Licensure Compact mises à jour, Champ d
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.continuingEd: Continuing éducation exigences are integral to licence renewal. Regulatory bodies periodically mettre à jour these exigences to adresse emerging soins de santé challenges, nouveau Fondé sur des preuves practices, and evolving Sécurité du patient standards.
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.eachDestinat: Each destination pays has established organizations responsible for evaluating the accréditations of internationally educated nurses. These evaluations assess whether your Formation en soins infirmiers and clinique experience meet local standards.
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.guideToInter: Guide to international infirmier recruitment policies including accréditation évaluation processes, visa and immigration pathways, bridging programme exigences, and recruitment agency regulations for internationally educated nurses.
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.healthcarePo: Soins de santé Politique & Mises à jour
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.healthcareRe: Soins de santé regulations shape every aspect of Pratique infirmière. Understanding regulatory developments helps you maintain compliance, advocate for your profession, and deliver sûr Soins aux patients.
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.healthcareWo: Soins de santé workplace sécurité regulations adresse unique risks faced by nurses including exposure to infectious maladies, workplace violence, musculo-squelettique injuries from patient handling, and psychological stressors. Récent legislative tendances inclure stronger protections against workplace violence and mandatory break periods.
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.internationa: International infirmier recruitment involves navigating immigration systèmes that are designed to adresse soins de santé workforce shortages. Many countries have créé specialized visa catégories for soins de santé professionals, recognizing the critique rôle nurses play in their soins de santé systèmes.
International Infirmier Recruitment Policies: Accréditation Évaluation, Visa Pathways & Bridging Programmes | NurseNest
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.manyJurisdic: Many jurisdictions exiger internationally educated nurses to complet bridging programmes that adresse gaps between their formation and local pratique standards. These programmes typically inclure supervised clinique placements, classroom instruction activé local soins de santé systèmes, and competency assessments.
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.navigateTheP: Naviguer the policies and pathways that govern international infirmier recruitment. From accréditation évaluation to visa traitement, understand the framework for cross-border infirmier carrières.
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.nurseStaffin: Infirmier(ère) staffing regulations are among the most impactful politique developments in soins de santé. Research consistently demonstrates the relationship between infirmier(ère) staffing niveaux and patient résultats, including mortality rates, hôpital-acquired infections, patient falls, and médicament erreurs.
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.nursingExamF: Examen infirmier Format Mises à jour
Examen infirmier Format Mises à jour: NGN, CAT Changes, Score Reporting & Testing Policies | NurseNest
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.nursingExami: Infirmier examinations evolve to better mesurer clinique competency. Understanding format changes, nouveau élément types, and scoring methodologies helps you préparer effectively.
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.nursingLicen: Infirmier Licensing Politique Changes: Compact Licenses, Champ d
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.recentTrends: Récent tendances inclure mandatory éducation activé opioid prescribing and douleur gestion, implicit bias and santé equity formation, infection prévention in the contexte of emerging maladies, and suicide prévention évaluation competencies.
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.regulatoryBo: Regulatory bodies continuously mettre à jour sécurité standards based activé Fondé sur des preuves pratique. Clé zones inclure médicament sécurité (barcode scanning, intelligent pump technology), infection prévention protocols, fall prévention programmes, pressure blessure prévention, and sûr patient handling exigences.
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.regulatoryCh: Regulatory Changes Affecting Nurses: Staffing Laws, Sécurité Standards & Pratique Exigences | NurseNest
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.scoreReporti: Score reporting processes have been streamlined in récent years. NCLEX résultats are typically disponible within 48 heures through Rapide Résultats, with official résultats sent to the candidate
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.stayCurrentW: Stay actuel with Examen infirmier format changes. Complet guide covering Suivant Generation NCLEX mises à jour, computerized adaptive testing changes, score reporting modifications, and testing center policies.
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.telehealthRe: Telehealth regulations have evolved rapidly, creating nouveau opportunities and challenges for Pratique infirmière. Clé regulatory considerations inclure cross-état pratique authority, informed consent for virtual soins, documentation exigences, and Champ d
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.theMinimumNu: The minimum numéro of questions varies by examen: NCLEX-RN requires a minimum of 85 éléments (maximum 150), while the NCLEX-PN requires a minimum of 85 éléments (maximum 150). The examen ends when the algorithm reaches sufficient confidence in the pass/fail decision or when the maximum numéro of éléments is reached.
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.theNextGener: The Suivant Generation NCLEX represents the most significant changer to infirmier licensure examinations in decades. NGN introduces Jugement clinique-focused élément types that go beyond traditional multiple-choice questions to assess how nurses think through complexe patient scénarios.
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.theNurseLice: The Infirmier(ère) Licensure Compact is one of the most significant politique developments in US infirmier. It allows nurses holding a multistate licence to pratique in any compact membre état without obtaining supplémentaire licenses. This has dramatically improved workforce mobility and disaster réponse capabilities.
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.theWhoGlobal: The WHO Global Code of Pratique activé the International Recruitment of Santé Personnel provides a framework for ethical recruitment. Clé principes inclure transparency in recruitment practices, fair traitement of internationally recruited staff, sustainability of santé systèmes in source countries, and prohibition of charging recruitment fees to candidates.
pages.healthcarePolicyPages.understandin: Understanding how licensure policies evolve is essentiel for maintaining your accréditations and planning carrière moves across jurisdictions. This guide covers the majeur politique frameworks affecting infirmier licenses.

pages.herbalSupplementsHub.askOpenendedW: Ask ouvrir-ended: "What supplements, vitamins, or herbal produits do you take?"

pages.herbalSupplementsHub.ginkgoGarlicG: Ginkgo, Garlic, Ginger, Ginseng — tous increase saignement risque. Examen writers love to tester a patient activé warfarin who is also taking one of these. Correct réponse: notifier the prescriber about additive saignement risque.

pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.05Mlkg: 0.5 mL/kg/hr over dernier 4 heures (borderline — normal minimum for children: 1 mL/kg/hr).
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.20Mlkg: 20 mL/kg NS bolus (14 kg × 20 = 280 mL) over 5–10 minutes. Reassess after each bolus — may répéter up to 60 mL/kg in the premier heure. Assess for liquide responsiveness: improved Fréquence cardiaque, perfusion, mental statut, urine sortie.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.30Mlkg: 30 mL/kg crystalloid (typically NS or LR) within 3 heures. For this 65 kg patient: ~2000 mL bolus. Reassess liquide responsiveness after each bolus.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.4Obtai: 4. Obtenez des cultures et des laboratoires :
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.5IfMap: 5. If CARTE remains < 65 mmHg after liquides:
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.7Posit: 7. Placez la surface de cuisson à 30 degrés
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.activa: Activer rapid réponse or pédiatrique Urgence équipe. This child shows signs of compensated septic choc — do not attendre for further deterioration.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.acuteE: Aigu exacerbation of COPD (AECOPD) with aigu-activé-chronique type II respiratoire échec. Yellow-green sputum and mild leukocytosis suggérer an infectious déclencheur (likely bacterial bronchitis or pneumonia).
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.acuteI: Aigu ischemic avc — likely cardioembolic origin (atrial fibrillation with subtherapeutic anticoagulation). Left middle cerebral artery (MCA) territory based activé right hemiparesis, facial droop, and aphasia modèle.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.acuteK: Aigu kidney blessure (creatinine 1.6 from référence 0.9), altered mental statut, hypotension, elevated lactate — multiple organe systèmes are showing dysfunction consistent with septicémie.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.acuteP: Aigu pericarditis (diffuse ST elevation, no reciprocal changes), aortic dissection (tearing douleur, unequal pulses), pulmonaire embolism (pleuritic douleur, tachycardia, hypoxia).
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.admitt: Admitted 18 heures ago with communauté-acquired pneumonia. Night infirmier(ère) rapports the child
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.alertB: Alerte but confused, follows simple commands inconsistently. Right hemiparesis (arm greater than leg). Right-sided facial droop (lower face). Receptive langue partially intact but expressive aphasia present. Right visual champ neglect suspected.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.alread: (already obtained: 3.8 mmol/L). Répéter lactate in 2–4 heures. Cible: lactate clearance or decrease by ≥ 10%.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.anxiou: Poitrine anxieuse, diaphorétique et serrée. Peau pâle et moite.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.atrial: Atrial fibrillation (activé warfarin, inconsistent compliance), hypertension, hyperlipidemia, type 2 diabetes.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.bilate: Bilateral breath sounds effacer, no crackles. Mild tachypnea.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.bloodC: Blood cultures × 2, CBC with différentiel, CRP, lactate, BMP (Électrolytes, glucose, rénal fonction), blood gas. Obtain before changing antibiotiques if possible.
Blood cultures × 2 (from different sites), urine culture, plaie culture from chirurgical site. Do not delay antibiotiques plus than 45 minutes for cultures.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.bornAt: Born at 35 weeks gestation, no significant néonatal complications. Up to date activé immunizations. No chronique conditions. No allergies.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.bp1488: BP 148/88 mmHg, HR 110 bpm, RR 32/min, SpO₂ 86% activé room air, Temp 37.8°C
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.bp1589: BP 158/94 mmHg, HR 102 bpm (regular), RR 22/min, SpO₂ 96% activé room air, Temp 37.1°C
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.bp1861: TA 186/102 mmHg, FC 88 bpm (irrégulièrement irrégulier), RR 18/min, SpO₂ 97 %, Temp 36,8 °C, Glycémie 142 mg/dL
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.bp8852: BP 88/52 mmHg (référence 130/78), HR 118 bpm, RR 24/min, SpO₂ 93% activé room air, Temp 39.2°C (102.6°F)
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.cardia: Cardiaque surveillance, continuous pouls oximetry, strict I&O (weigh diapers), frequent Signes vitaux q15min during aigu resuscitation, blood glucose surveillance (children are prone to hypoglycemia during septicémie).
Cardiaque telemetry, serial ECGs, répéter troponin at 3 and 6 heures, strict I&O.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.cefazo: Cefazolin 2g IV q8h (chirurgical prophylaxis), enoxaparin 40 mg SC daily, oxycodone 5 mg PO q4h PRN, metformin (held publier-op), lisinopril 10 mg daily.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.childr: Children compensate for choc much longer than adults, maintaining Tension artérielle until very late. By the temps hypotension occurs in a child, they are in decompensated choc — a pre-arrest état. The clé is recognizing early signs: tachycardia, altered mental statut, poor perfusion, and decreased urine sortie.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.confus: Confus, agité, semble malade. Peau chaude et rouge avec une faible turgescence.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.contin: Albutérol nébulisé en continu 2,5 mg + ipratropium 0,5 mg toutes les 20 minutes × 3, puis toutes les 4 heures. Envisagez d'ajouter du sulfate de magnésium IV en cas de bronchospasme sévère.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.copdDi: COPD (diagnosed 8 years ago), 40 pack-année smoking historique (actuel smoker), hypertension, anxiety. Two prior COPD exacerbations requiring hospitalization in the past année.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.ctComp: CT complété within 10 minutes of arrival (achieved). Règle out hemorrhagic avc before considering thrombolytics.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.ctHead: Tête CT (sans contraste, obtenue en 10 minutes) :
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.decrea: Decreased cardiaque sortie connexe to myocardial ischemia/infarction as evidenced by ST elevation, elevated troponin, chest douleur, tachycardia, and diaphoresis.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.dimini: Une diminution de la respiration retentit bilatéralement avec des respirations sifflantes et des ronchis expiratoires épars. Phase expiratoire prolongée. Configuration de la poitrine en tonneau. Pas de stridor.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.doorto: — door-to-balloon temps objectif < 90 minutes for primaire PCI.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.dose09: Dose: 0.9 mg/kg (max 90 mg). Give 10% as IV bolus over 1 minute, then infuse restant 90% over 60 minutes. Commencer within 60 minutes of arrival.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.explai: Expliquer procédures, provide emotional soutenir, garder family informed.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.faceLe: Face — left-sided facial droop when smiling. Arms — right arm drifts downward and cannot maintain against gravity. Speech — dysarthric, mot-finding difficulté, Impossible de répéter sentences accurately. Temps — symptôme onset 30 minutes ago.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.foleyC: Foley cathéter in place — urine cloudy and concentrated, sortie 15 mL in dernier 2 heures.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.forTpa: For tPA candidates, BP must be < 185/110 before and < 180/105 during/after tPA. Actuel BP 186/102 — administrer IV labetalol 10–20 mg to lower BP below threshold.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.hepari: Heparin bolus + drip per protocol, clopidogrel/ticagrelor chargement dose, beta-blocker (metoprolol) if HR > 100 and no contre-indications.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.hr168B: HR 168 bpm (normal for age: 80–120), RR 42/min (normal: 20–30), SpO₂ 90% activé 2L nasal cannula (was 96% activé admission), Temp 39.8°C (103.6°F), BP 78/45 mmHg (normal minimum SBP: 70 + [2 × age] = 76 mmHg — borderline faible)
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.hypert: Hypertension (10 years), hyperlipidemia, type 2 diabetes, former smoker (quit 5 years ago), family historique of MI (father at age 58).
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.iHaveA: "I have a crushing douleur in my chest that won
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.increa: Increase to élevé-flow nasal cannula or non-rebreather mask to cible SpO₂ > 94%. Position upright. Be prepared for respiratoire soutenir (CPAP, intubation) if grunting worsens or mental statut declines further.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.initia: Initiate empiric antibiotiques (e.g., azithromycin or levofloxacin) given purulent sputum and suspected bacterial déclencheur.
Initiate vasopressor thérapie (norepinephrine premier-line). May exiger ICU transférer.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.inr16S: INR 1.6 (subtherapeutic — cible 2.0–3.0 activé warfarin), glucose 142 mg/dL, CBC within normal limits.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.keepNp: Garder NPO until bedside swallow évaluation complété. Dysphagia screening is mandatory before any oral intake (aspiration risque).
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.keepPa: Garder parents informed and at bedside when possible. Expliquer interventions and what to expect. Parents know their child meilleur — their concern that the child "doesn
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.lastKn: Dernier known well temps 30 minutes ago — well within the 4.5-heure window for IV alteplase. CT shows no hémorragie. INR 1.6 (alteplase can be given if INR < 1.7). No récent surgery, saignement, or autre absolute contre-indications identified.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.lisino: Lisinopril 20 mg par jour, atorvastatine 40 mg par jour, metformine 1 000 mg deux fois par jour.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.listle: Listless, not making eye contact, not interested in toys. Responds to voice but irritable when touched. Not crying with tears — mucous membranes tacky. Skin mottled activé extremities, capillary refill 4 secondes.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.methyl: Methylprednisolone 125 mg IV or prednisone 40 mg PO. Reduces voies aériennes inflammation and shortens récupération temps.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.miaWas: Mia was admitted with 3-jour historique of cough, fièvre, and decreased oral intake. Initial chest X-ray showed right lower lobe consolidation. Started activé IV ampicillin. Was playful and interactif activé admission but has become progressively less actif over the past 6 heures. Parents rapport she has not eaten and has had minimal liquide intake.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.morphi: Morphine 2–4 mg IV PRN (if douleur not relieved by NTG), Oxygène only if SpO₂ < 94%, Nitroglycerin 0.4 mg SL q5min × 3 (hold if SBP < 90), Aspirin 325 mg chewed (already taken by patient).
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.neuroC: Neuro checks q15min × 2h, then q30min × 6h, then q1h × 16h. Strict BP surveillance — garder < 180/105. Moniteur for signs of hemorrhagic conversion (nouveau headache, vomiting, aigu neuro refuser).
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.noHemo: No hémorragie identified. No early ischemic changes.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.normal: Normal BNP règles out cœur échec as a contributing factor. The clinique picture is consistent with infectious COPD exacerbation.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.notify: Notifier neurologist/stroke équipe immediately. Préparer for IV alteplase (tPA) administration.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.nursin: Infirmier Scénarios cliniques — Interactif Études de cas
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.obtain: Obtain before starting antibiotiques if possible.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.osteoa: Osteoarthritis, hypertension, type 2 diabetes (A1C 7.8%), urinaire tract infections (recurrent).
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.painSt: Douleur started 45 minutes ago while watching television. Substernal, radiating to the left arm and jaw. Rates douleur 8/10. Describes it as "heavy" and "squeezing." Associated with diaphoresis and nausea. Took one aspirin at accueil before calling 911.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.patien: Patient was at référence 30 minutes ago. Wife rapports he suddenly dropped his fork, his right arm went limp, and he began speaking incoherently. He is able to suivre simple commands inconsistently. Dernier known well temps: 12:15 PM. Actuel temps: 12:45 PM.

Patient was progressing normally activé POD 1 and 2. Activé POD 3 morning, she became increasingly confused and oriented only to person. Temperature spiked to 39.2°C. She rapports feeling "cold" despite the fièvre. Chirurgical plaie has increased redness and warmth around incision edges.

Patient with known COPD (GOLD Étape III) presents to the ED with worsening dyspnea over the past 3 jours. Rapports increased sputum production (yellow-green) and a productive cough. Used his rescue inhaler 8 times aujourd
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.ph731A: pH 7.31 = acidosis. PaCO₂ 58 = elevated (respiratoire acidosis). HCO₃ 32 = elevated (chronique compensation). PaO₂ 54 = severe hypoxemia. This is a partially compensated respiratoire acidosis with aigu-activé-chronique respiratoire échec. The elevated HCO₃ indicates pre-existing chronique CO₂ retention (référence COPD) with aigu worsening.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.ph731P: pH 7,31, PaCO₂ 58 mmHg, HCO₃ 32 mEq/L, PaO₂ 54 mmHg
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.postop: Publier-operative jour 3 following right total hip arthroplasty. Infirmier(ère) noted increasing confusion and elevated temperature during routine évaluation.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.practi: Pratique Raisonnement clinique with interactif infirmier scénarios. Chest douleur, septicémie, respiratoire distress, avc, and pédiatrique deterioration cases with évaluation findings, infirmier priorities, and quiz questions.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.repeat: Répéter ABG in 1–2 heures. If PaCO₂ continues to rise, pH drops below 7.25, or mental statut worsens → préparer for non-invasive ventilation (BiPAP) or intubation. BiPAP is premier-line for COPD exacerbation with respiratoire acidosis.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.rightH: Right hip incision with erythema extending 3 cm beyond staple line, warmth, and small montant of purulent drainage.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.s1s2He: S1/S2 entendu, pas de murmure. Tachycardie régulière. Pas de JVD.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.sittin: Sitting upright, tripod position, using accessory muscles (sternocleidomastoid, intercostals), pursed-lip breathing, speaks 2–3 mot sentences only. Appears fatigued.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.stElev: Élévation ST dans les dérivations II, III, aVF (2–3 mm). Dépression réciproque ST dans les dérivations I et aVL.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.startO: Commencer O₂ via Venturi mask at 28% — titrate to cible SpO₂ 88–92%. COPD patients rely activé hypoxic drive; excessive O₂ can suppress respiratoire drive and worsen CO₂ retention.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.strict: Strict I&O (urine sortie objectif ≥ 0.5 mL/kg/hr), continuous cardiaque surveillance, frequent Signes vitaux (q15 min during resuscitation), neuro checks q1h.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.subcos: Subcostal and intercostal retractions, nasal flaring, grunting activé expiration. Diminished breath sounds right lower lobe with crackles. Increased travail of breathing compared to admission.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.surgic: Chirurgical site infection (SSI) with purulent drainage and expanding erythema. Concurrent UTI possible given cloudy urine, indwelling cathéter, and historique of recurrent UTIs. Les deux sources exiger cultures before broadening antibiotique couverture.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.tachyc: Tachycardic, faible peripheral pulses. Cool extremities despite fièvre.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.theCli: The clinique picture suggests progression from localized pneumonia to possible septicémie with impending septic choc. The hémodynamique changes (tachycardia, borderline BP, poor perfusion) suggérer compensated choc — the corps is maintaining Tension artérielle through tachycardia, but decompensation is imminent if not treated aggressively.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.theCur: The actuel regimen (ampicillin alone) may be insufficient. Notifier the fournisseur to consider broadening to ceftriaxone or adding vancomycin if MRSA is suspected.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.theStE: The ST elevation in inferior leads (II, III, aVF) with reciprocal changes in lateral leads indicates an inferior STEMI. The elevated troponin confirms myocardial blessure. Facteurs de risque (age, male, HTN, DM, hyperlipidemia, smoking historique, family historique) créer a élevé pretest probability for ACS. The 45-minute symptôme durée places this patient within the PCI window.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.thisCh: This child is showing multiple red flags for clinique deterioration: rising Fréquence cardiaque, worsening respiratoire distress (retractions, grunting, nasal flaring), declining oxygène saturation despite supplemental O₂, altered behavior (listless, not interactif), poor perfusion (mottled skin, prolonged cap refill, cool extremities, faible pulses), decreased urine sortie, and persistent élevé fièvre.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.thisPa: This patient meets SIRS criteria (temp > 38.3°C, HR > 90, RR > 20, WBC > 12,000) with a suspected infection source (chirurgical site and/or UTI). The qSOFA score of 2 (altered mentation + hypotension) indicates Risque élevé for poor résultat. Elevated lactate (3.8 mmol/L) indicates tissu hypoperfusion — this patient has septicémie with organe dysfunction progressing toward septic choc.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.timeIs: "Temps is brain" — approximately 1.9 million neurons are lost per minute during an aigu ischemic avc. Every minute of delay in reperfusion worsens résultats. Door-to-needle temps objectif: < 60 minutes.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.tiotro: Tiotropium (Spiriva) 18 mcg par jour, fluticasone/salmétérol (Advair) 250/50 BID, albutérol (Ventolin) PRN, lisinopril 10 mg par jour.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.toOpti: to optimiser cerebral perfusion and reduce ICP risque.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.tropon: Troponine I : 0,8 ng/mL (élevé), BNP : 180 pg/mL, glucose : 185 mg/dL.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.twoLar: Deux IV de gros calibre (18G ou plus). Laboratoires de dessin : troponine, CK-MB, CBC, BMP, études de coagulation, typage et dépistage.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.warfar: Warfarine 5 mg par jour, métoprolol 50 mg deux fois par jour, atorvastatine 80 mg par jour, metformine 500 mg deux fois par jour.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.wbc182: WBC 18,200/μL, lactate 3.8 mmol/L, creatinine 1.6 mg/dL (référence 0.9), glucose 245 mg/dL, procalcitonin 4.2 ng/mL.
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.wifeCa: La femme a appelé le 911 pour signaler que son mari avait soudainement développé une faiblesse du côté droit et que « son discours ne fonctionnait pas ».
pages.nursingClinicalScenariosHub.within: within 1 heure of septicémie recognition. Notifier surgeon and infectious maladie. Broaden from cefazolin to cover les deux SSI and UTI sources (e.g., piperacillin-tazobactam + vancomycin).
